summary: Abyss Ascendant — a dungeon RPG with character selection, combat tick system, and monster encounters. Currently fixing HP preservation on character select and attack tick rate defaults.
architectural_notes: Combat uses a tick-based system; player default is 12 ticks per attack, enemies start at 21 ticks (level 1) and decrease by 1 tick every 5 levels
Only tackle explicitly requested changes — do not add or remove features between updates without user instruction
known_issues: Character HP resets to max when backing out to character select screen instead of preserving current HP
Player was attacking every tick instead of every 12 ticks — tick rate was changed without user request

existing_features.title: When a player backs out to the character select screen and re-selects their character, the character's current HP must be preserved exactly as it was — it must NOT reset to max HP. The HP value should be saved (either to backend or persistent local state) before navigating away from the dungeon/game screen and restored when re-entering with that character.

Fix the player attack tick rate so the player attacks every 12 ticks by default (not every tick). Additionally, set level 1 enemies to attack every 21 ticks, scaling down by 1 tick every 5 enemy levels (level 5 = 20 ticks, level 10 = 19 ticks, level 15 = 18 ticks, etc.). These values must be defined as named constants in the combat engine and must not be changed unless explicitly requested.

new_feature_requests.summary: HP preservation on character select: save and restore character's current HP when backing out and re-entering
Attack tick rate fix: player attacks every 12 ticks by default; level 1 enemies attack every 21 ticks, scaling down by 1 tick every 5 enemy levels
